content.problemStatement:
Chef has decided to go to a gold mine along with NNN of his friends (thus, total N+1N+1N+1 people including Chef go to the gold mine).

The gold mine contains a total of XXX kg of gold. Every person has the capacity of carrying up atmost\textbf{atmost}atmost YYY kg of gold.

Will Chef and his friends together be able to carry up all the gold from the gold mine assuming that they can go to the mine exactly once.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains of a single line of input, three integers N,X,YN, X, YN,X,Y.


Output Format
For each testcase, output `"YES"` if you and your friends can carry all the gold, otherwise output `"NO"`.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings "yEs", "yes", "Yes" and "YES" will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers N, X and Y — the number of Chef's friends, the total kilograms of gold in the mine and the maximum kilograms each person can carry.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if Chef and his friends together can carry all the gold in one trip, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: capacity = (2+1)*3 = 9 < 10 → NO.\nTest case 2: capacity = (2+1)*4 = 12 ≥ 10 → YES.\nTest case 3: capacity = (1+1)*10 = 20 ≥ 5 → YES.
Only Chef is present. Capacity = (0+1)*7 = 7 which equals the gold amount 7 → YES.

solution.approach: For each test case compute the total carrying capacity as (N + 1) * Y because Chef and N friends are (N+1) people. If this capacity is greater than or equal to X, the gold can be carried, otherwise it cannot. This is a direct O(1) check per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it)); X = int(next(it)); Y = int(next(it))
        capacity = (N + 1) * Y
        out.append('YES' if capacity >= X else 'NO')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
